untranslatable_reason: Point-and-figure charting requires explicit resistance/support level definitions and penetration thresholds that are marked AUTHOR_SILENT. The method depends on constructing P&F charts with box sizes and reversal amounts that cannot be derived from standard price/volume indicators. Resistance/support levels, penetration amounts, and stop-loss placement rules are all point-and-figure-specific constructs not available in the allowed indicator set (which contains only price derivatives, moving averages, and momentum oscillators). Without quantifiable threshold values for these parameters, no actionable rule can be generated.
translation_notes: Signal requires point-and-figure chart construction and manual level identification. The allowed indicators cannot synthesize P&F resistance/support penetration signals. To make this translatable, the signal would need: (1) explicit penetration_threshold value, (2) resistance_level_definition formula (e.g., 'highest high of last 20 bars'), (3) support_level_definition formula (e.g., 'lowest low of last 20 bars'), and (4) stop-loss placement rule expressed numerically.
